I have been trying to see how I can make a team that cause the most Final Verdict possible.
That mean: Eternal Loom the most often possible, and optimize Arachne's Exalt.

The team look like that

Daffodil is a key to the strategy:
- Using her Exalt when there is no Thousand Mirage in the ultra slot make you skip one turn of wait before Singularity. Arachne's Rouse can replace a Thousand Mirage every 3 turn with another command card, so Daff can Exalt once more to fill yet another slot, and effectively turn Singularity into a every-three-rounds things
- With E1, Strike and Defense cost nothing on Leap, this is very useful to optimize the Singularity rounds, where you have only 5 arithm, but might want more to make more than 5 pursuits. Also, Defense stacks permanent STR on E1
- Thousand Mirage can provide many useful effect for that start: fetching more Eternal Loom, stack STR, restore Arith
I did put Rewind Time on her because she's not the main DPS, even if she does do decent damage: the goal is that she make always one more Strike/Defense during Singularity, making it 6 command cards played for 0 cost. (6 pursuits min, with the right setup)

Winkle is mainly here as a shield/aliemus support, it's common I use her Psyche Reforged on turn 2 to clear the hand from all clutter cards that could annoy me during singularity (turn 3 if everything plays well), which securing my shields

Horla is also here to help with Aliemus.
Originally she hoarded Arithmetic and cards for ultra round, but Singularity reset the Arithm now, and it seems even the card hoarding doesn't work well recently (did a patch happen modifying this?)
She allowed me in the first day to go up to 8 pursuit during a single Singularity.

It's difficult to envision to not use 2 Aliemus supports here, because the strat relies a lot on Exalts.

.
- Did someone tested Horla +Archane and can tell me if Singularity now reset the hand despite of Horla's Happyness effect?
- Do you see a wait to push the combination farther with the goal to generate even more Verdicts?
you might die later
not too relevant though for your first 9 chapters as you can probably just wing through
the later chapters may force you a gnosis or two if you want to stick to that, which wouldnt matter anyway
are you asking if the 0 cost will be deleted at end of turn?
Reset the hand? What does that mean
yeah it will get deleted even if ur in joy stance
Fleeting just disappears right?

Horla's Joy has not relation with Fleeting => it gives all command cards "Retain".

On the normal Ultra, the Ultra Round is a new round, so all Retain card are kept, and the cards in the Ultra slots drawn on top of them, which result, with Horla, in Ultra rounds with a hand of 10 cards.

Now, with Singularity, the "Retain" cards are flushed out and replaced by the cards in the Ultra slot, reducing the hand to 5 cards, and limiting the number of pursuits
My feeling of confusion is that I'm almost sure that the first days, card "Retained" by Horla's Joy were not flushed... have I been wrong?
they changed it
Was it made on purpose? Like, announced in the patch notes?
Yes
Weird, I don't understand that choice. Other Retain cards work normaly (like Arachne's 3 stays in hand normally)
But it might question my choice to use Horla as a 4th unit
Is it just for the Joy effect? Does the Retain of her E2 works during Singularity transition?

Retain keyword on card = retain
No keyword = no retain
That's pretty much it
I don't have Horla's E2 to see if the keyword is added like with some other enlighten effects
Her E2 adds the keyword, yeh
ok, so maybe at E2 she could keep her spot, but I'm far from that

E1 is the minimum for her, but she does feel considerably better to play at OE, yeh
And the bonus counter/poison buff from OE is also quite beneficial for Daff

So, a little more question about the strategy I try to improve.

The goal is to perform a 3-turn cycle of Ultra Singularity with Daffodil and Arachne, by using this method:
- **Turn 1 & 2:** Try to complete Arachne's Arouse and one activation of Daffodil's Exalt, to end with a Ultra bar with 2 priority command cards* and one Thousand Mirages
- **Turn 3:** Annihilate the Thousand Mirages, and cast Arachne's Exalt to trigger pursuits when I replace the Mirages by another priority command card, yet another priority command card (4 slots full), and Daffodil's Exalt once more (5 slots full)
- **Extra Turn:** Get the priority cards stored and get more Weaver stacks and trigger more pursuits

**"Priority commands cards"* means for me, by priority Eternal Loon (to get more Weaver stack during singurality), Daffodil E1's Defense(cost 0, stacks shield, aliemus, keyflare and STR), Daffodil E1's Strike (cost 0, but still generate aliemus and keyflare)

My question is mainly about the wheels/covenants: what should I aim to make this cycle go smother?
fix the covs, if daff is skey give her life drain. or, if you want her to do damage give her damage wods.
horla wants burial to go with dr wods
i would give winkle the aliemus cov so you can exalt faster
dreams arcane prolly better
wut does horla do in that team
My keeper level isn't 58 yet, so that covenant is locked, and dream of medicine too
Pump up the aliemus generation, as Daffodil need to exalt twice every 3 turns
Horla covenant is currently a 3 piece burial 3 piece twins, to maximize her permanent DR. She doesn't need the 6-piece bonus of either set

current team and what i have atm, what teams could i make with this that are good?

Liz is strong... in the right context. Those aren't unit that support her well.

Liz is an unit that want to monopolize the most amount of card slot you have... in hand, and in the ultra bar.
Horla's E2 will make her card stay in hand, and compete with Liz cards
Winkle's Reforged Psyche will flush your hand.
Arachne is an oustanding support, but not very specifically good for Liz.

So, I'd advise to change either your DPS, or change the supports, you struggle with non-synergizing units here.
